results.rendered_html: vi-śeṣya n. (in gram.) the word to be ‘differenced’ or distinguished (from another word which is called vi-śeṣaṇa, q.v.), a substantive, noun, the object or subject of a predicate,  Pāṇ. ;  Tarkas. ;  Vedāntas.

results.rendered_html:
viśeṣya, viśeṣaṇīya a.
1 To be distinguished.
2 Chief, superior.
— ṣyaṃ The word qualified or limited by an adjective, the object to be defined or particularized by another word; a noun; viśeṣyaṃ nābhidhā gacchetkṣīṇaśaktirviśeṣaṇe  K. P. 2.
